baseline_note: Every persistence benchmark expects compile-ready GRDB code. No N+1 queries. All database access stays in the Data layer. Migrations are append-only and use versioned identifiers.

evals.prompt: Set up a GRDB database with an initial migration for an Expense table with id, amount, date, and category fields
evals.expected_output: DatabaseManager with DatabasePool, registerMigrations with v1 migration defining the expense table
expectations: Implements DatabaseManager with DatabasePool and a makeShared() factory
Registers a v1 migration with the expense table schema
Table has at least: id (text primary key), amount (integer or real), date (datetime), category (text)
Migration identifier is versioned (v1)
Code is compile-ready Swift

evals.prompt: Add a migration to attach tags to expenses — I want to store a list of tag names per expense
evals.expected_output: Append-only versioned migration (v2+) adding a tags table or JSON column without modifying v1
expectations: Uses a new versioned migration identifier (v2 or higher)
Does not modify or re-register the existing v1 migration
Produces compile-ready Swift migration code

evals.prompt: The expense list view should update automatically in real time whenever I add, edit, or delete an expense
evals.expected_output: ValueObservation in ViewModel with async for-in loop and Task lifecycle management
expectations: Uses ValueObservation.tracking to observe the expense table
Iterates values with for try await inside a Task
Cancels the observation Task in deinit or a stopObserving() method
Code is compile-ready Swift

evals.prompt: Write a query that returns total amount spent per category for a given month — the app shows a summary chart
evals.expected_output: Single SQL query with GROUP BY and SUM mapped to a typed projection row — no Swift aggregation loops
expectations: Uses a single SQL query with GROUP BY and SUM (or GRDB aggregate expression)
Does not issue one query per category (no N+1 pattern)
Maps results to a typed FetchableRecord projection struct
Code is compile-ready Swift

evals.prompt: Set up GRDB associations so I can fetch each Category with all of its Expenses in one efficient call
evals.expected_output: hasMany on CategoryRecord, belongsTo on ExpenseRecord, CategoryWithExpenses struct using including(all:)
expectations: Defines hasMany association on CategoryRecord pointing to ExpenseRecord
Defines belongsTo association on ExpenseRecord pointing to CategoryRecord
Uses including(all: CategoryRecord.expenses) to prefetch in two queries (not N+1)
Defines a decodable CategoryWithExpenses struct
Code is compile-ready Swift
